title: When Status changes to DONE, Remove due date / date
excerpt: I would like to remove a due date from an item when the status changes to DONE without archiving it. Is that possible? I’m creating multiple automations based on due date, but I don’t want them to affect an item that’s b…

posts.author: jelitots (Jo Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to remove a due date from an item when the status changes to DONE without archiving it. Is that possible? I’m creating multiple automations based on due date, but I don’t want them to affect an item that’s been completed. Thanks.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jelitots - welcome to the monday.com community! I’d love to learn a little more about your use case. Is this a recurring task that you’d like to continue changing the due date for?

I’m not sure we have exactly what you’re looking for in terms of automations. Would it help to push the due date out by a number of days or to create a new item with these details (minus the due date) instead?

image736×542 17.7 KB
image730×538 14.8 KB

posts.author: embloomquist (Eric Bloomquist)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there. I would also love this automation. For me the situation is this: I have a task set with a due date that is synced to my calendar. Once I change the status to Done it moves to the Done group with an automation, but because the due date is still attached to that item, it continues to stay synced to my calendar. I don’t want it to be archived yet or deleted (I want to do that myself as I review the tasks that I have completed), but I don’t want it on my calendar anymore, and it certainly doesn’t need a due date anymore if it’s a task that I completed myself.

I hope that you are able to add this automation soon. It would be quite helpful.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great suggestion, I have the same issue. Items that are Done but have a date in the future, still show in the google calendar as To be done. This is confusing.

My suggestion for a new automation would be:

When status X change to X, clear column X from this item.

This would be applicable to the Date and People column as wel. Also see When Status changes to DONE, Remove "Assignee" from People and Unassign when issue is set to done

posts.author: nigel (nigel reece)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, is there any news on this? I’d like to be able to remove the ‘due date’ from a job, when it is marked as completed too. If you could update us taht would be great. thanks

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: @lauralev @Julia-monday.com @automation-team any update on this automation? It seems so trivial to implement and such a big time saver for a lot of users. It also keeps your ‘My week’ and ‘Everything assigned on me’ clean.

posts.author: jjay.lewin (Jake Jay-Lewin)
posts.cookedHtml: plus one to this feature request!

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
So far, custom Automations only allows you in the actions to set a date to “today”.

You can push it by X days, but not clear it yet.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, Tom pointed out that removing assignees by changing a status column is possible now Unassign when issue is set to done - #16 by samtoms

Maybe it does work with dates columns, I haven’t checked that yet.
I have an automation on the date column that after changing the status to done, it sets the current date and time. Works for me, now i know when it was solved.

posts.author: MaxCoelho (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this request!

Any news on this feature?

posts.author: Maplebrook (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes PLEASE.
GREAT idea for recurring tasks!

How do we make this happen?
How long will this take?
Thank you

posts.author: th3p3arl (Earl Dauley)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday team, pushing this again. Guaranteed others want this feature too and just haven’t posted about it yet. And this isn’t the only thread for this request. Is there a roadmap?

My use case is that I change status to “stuck” while I’m awaiting info from a client. At that point, my project won’t continue until I hear from them, so I don’t need a set due date. I’d love to have an automation to clear the due date column or set the value to zero.

Please advise!

posts.author: nikkilittle (NiColette Little)
posts.cookedHtml: This is much needed! You’vd got my vote.

posts.author: th3p3arl (Earl Dauley)
posts.cookedHtml: Checking in again. Monday team, do we have a plan for this?

posts.author: th3p3arl (Earl Dauley)
posts.cookedHtml: @melissav-monday.com

posts.author: jgauthier (Jordan)
posts.cookedHtml: Bump - would love this feature. We have a use case where once a status moves to Done/Completed/Green/etc. the due date should be set to blank, null, empty, etc.

posts.author: toleary (Trevor O’Leary)
posts.cookedHtml:
Was a solution to this problem ever figured? I am looking for a way to remove a date range from a timeline column once a status is changed to “done”.

Thanks!

posts.author: th3p3arl (Earl Dauley)
posts.cookedHtml: Nope - we’re still waiting. @Julia-monday.com @melissav-monday.com is there someplace else we should be asking for this? 2,700 views on this post in the past year

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@th3p3arl @toleary @jgauthier @nikkilittle

I can provide a custom built solution to this missing feature, if having this ability is an important part of your workflow. Unfortunately this feature has been requested/mentioned in multiple other post, not just this one. If its in the works by Monday, I haven’t seen an ETA.

If anyone is interested reply here or message me directly.

posts.author: roll (Evgeny Karev)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would extend this feature request to more broad:

“When status changes, clear value in a column”

My task is not related to dates I just need to remove “Points/Dates” value and ATM I can only set it to 0 (and I need it to be empty)

posts.author: mrosi (Matthew Rosidivito)
posts.cookedHtml: Wanted to add my voice in here for a feature request. I have a very built-out recruitment board that many of us link with our calendars to track start dates of new-hires. When a candidate declines an offer, I would like to clear the start date so that it does not show up on our calendar. Custom automations seem to only allow me to set the date to today or push the date by x.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
While I realize that this should ideally be handled by a native monday․com automation, for the moment your best bet if you need the functionality now is to use Integromat. Integromat isn’t only for connecting monday․com to other systems. You can use it as a mini-automation engine to enhance what monday․com can do. Send the data out to Integromat, do what you need to with it, then write back to the same item or other items.

~ Deb Cinkus
Product-certified Partner

Want to chat live with other monday․com fans & users?
Join our unofficial community chat on Discord: The Monday Neighborhood

posts.author: AmandaHarms (Amanda Harms)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please! We have non-recurring tasks setup with 2 dates: task date and final due date. Our task date is set in deadline mode and such is marked out when our status changes to Done. However, we cannot set our final due date to deadline or it will show up as a duplicate in My Week section and in Calendars. Would like to clear a date from a column once status is changed to X.

posts.author: enyaw (Wayne Costigan)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 It’s August 2021 and we’re still waiting! We need this more than ever!!

We’ve built a board to track Tax filings for our company with various reminders set to ensure that documents are being filed. Any time a task is set to 'Done" it would be incredibly helpful for the date to be removed so that they’re not then reminded of a task they’ve already completed - thanks!

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml:
“When status changes, clear Column” would be a good universal solution that would fit many needs including this one.

Edit: Just got the info that this kind of automation should be released this year.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great to hear that this will be included in the base automations.

In the meantime, for those who are interested, here’s a quick video on how easy it is to use Integromat to do this.

Jim

posts.author: Michaela_Staffova (Michaela)
posts.cookedHtml: This is some truly awesome stuff, @JCorrell! Great job! Not only is this super helpful, it is also really well explained. You clearly know your monday/Integromat

posts.author: toleary (Trevor O’Leary)
posts.cookedHtml: Has this automation been released yet? We are looking to remove dates when a task is marked done as well. Thanks in advance!

posts.author: Marc1 (Marc Rosser)
posts.cookedHtml: Not yet. I’ll try and get some info from Monday next week for the "When status changes, clear Column” automation.

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml: Sounds like it’s close. Another person banking on this feature.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Excellent Team created a small free app that can be used in any custom automation.

The installation link is monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

image1653×143 14.8 KB

posts.author: inesam (Inês Mendes)
posts.cookedHtml: Waiting for this feature too. Any news?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @inesam

It’s there, see above announcement of the free app “Clear the Column”, it can be used with any trigger (like status change) in custom automations.

posts.author: inesam (Inês Mendes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi basdebruin. Is this an app made by the Monday team? I see the warning “This app hasn’t been reviewed or approved by Monday”. Also, only admins can install it and I guess it would install it for everyone using Monday.
At my company there are security requirements for this type of thing so I would rather see an official feature improvement be released by Monday.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The app is created by Excellent Team, not monday.com. It runs as part of the monday.com apps framework.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Announcement for everyone if you havent seen it yet (may be for those of us who have enabled it in monday.labs) but there is a new automation action called “Clear Column”!

posts.cookedHtml:
Announcement for everyone if you havent seen it yet (may be for those of us who have enabled it in monday.labs) but there is a new automation action called “Clear Column”!

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)

posts.author: tdmorin (Trevor)
posts.cookedHtml: Here I thought I was a complete monday expert and you just blew my mind sir. Thank you for sharing your knowledge!
